transcript: Yeah, I didn't think you were listenin' to me. I said, I changed over from AT&T to MCI. I know you represent US West, but, uh... you also represent AT&T, right?
Yeah. But why... why, what happened?

Well, I changed over to MCI. Then I found out all of this pornography, that MCI is supporting on TV, and I knocked 'em off. And I went back to AT&T, and now I found that AT&T supports almost as much pornography as MCI. And, uh... I'm getting real upset with these outfits, that all they can do is support pornography on TV... for all the kids to watch.
[laughs] In other words, you like pornography, is that it?
You don't know whether or not you've watched pornography on the TV-I? I...
I've never... I've never seen it.
Oh... Man, if you don't know that, I don't know what I'm doin' talkin' to you, now.
I, uh... I really-- I really can't say either way, Henry. Uh... I, and, uh...
And you still don't know what pornography is?
Well, I don't know what you can do about it, because you represent US West, and I don't know who US West advertises with.
And you don't like... you don't like what you're seeing, is that it?
I don't like what I'm seein'. I don't... I've got a five year old and a two year old grandchild.

And they're brought up to the point where, we have to continually watch, uh... TV, every program that they start to turn on, we have to watch it. Because they even got pornography now... for little kids.
That's what they're starting out at.
Yes. For kids. For sexual activity among little children. For nakedness. For bare-breastedness. For sellin' gays... and lesbians on TV. Uh uh. It's not right.
It's wrong, is that what you're saying?
The morality in this country is going to pot. And that's not what I fought World War II and Korea for... about.
Uh huh. [exhale] Well, I, uh... I feel your pain, Henry.
